items.title: Sweeping cuts but little detail — can the government stick to the Abetz budget?

children.content: Since Eric Abetz became Tasmania's treasurer nine months ago, there have been two prevailing questions. How much will be cut? And what will be cut? As of yesterday, we know how much, but don't know what or where.

articles.title: Government to slash 1,700 jobs as Abetz looks to rein in Tasmanian spending

children.content: The Tasmanian government will slash 1,700 jobs through natural attrition and negotiated voluntary redundancies, as Treasurer Eric Abetz unveils his second state budget.

image.altText: Treasurer Eric Abetz in front of a budget cuts sign and piles of money.

articles.title: 'Hard to achieve, in our opinion': Ratings agency unimpressed with Tas budget

children.content: Credit rating agency S&P Global says Tasmania's budget repair ambitions face "high execution risk". The government aims to save $1.5 billion through cuts.

image.altText: Eric Abetz speaking while Jeremy Rockliff listens.

gallery.altText: Tasmanian Treasurer Eric Abetz and Premier Jeremy Rockliff in the budget lock-up for the 2026-27 state budget.

thumbnail.altText: Tasmanian Treasurer Eric Abetz and Premier Jeremy Rockliff in the budget lock-up for the 2026-27 state budget.

items.title: Dismay over 150-year-old house being reduced to rubble

children.content: How did a colonial-era mansion once earmarked to be on the state heritage register get bulldozed?

image.altText: An old black and white photograph of a house at 180 George Street, Launceston.

items.title: Public transport to remain free in Tasmania for an additional year

children.content: The Tasmanian government has committed $24 million in the state budget towards keeping buses and ferries free for one more year.

image.altText: Tasmanian Government announce that free public transport will be extended by a year 2026-05-21 10:05:00

items.title: What's being cut in Tasmania's state budget?

children.content: Treasurer Eric Abetz wants to find $1.47 billion in "efficiencies" in Tasmania's state budget. This is what's on the chopping block.

gallery.altText: Tas budget cuts cards graphic showing six categories.

thumbnail.altText: Tas budget cuts cards graphic showing six categories.

items.title: Your state budget questions answered

children.content: Here's what you wanted us to find out from the Tasmanian budget:
How is this unprecedented debt going to be paid for?
What about the stadium?
What cost of living relief is there?
What education commitments are there?
How will the budget impact Tasmania's credit rating?
Reporter Selina Bryan brings you the answers.

image.altText: BudgetExplainTHUMB

items.title: Images show last-known movements of alleged New Town murder victim

children.content: Police release images showing the last known public movements of a Hobart woman who was found dead in her home almost two months later.

items.title: They’re black, white and very cute. But not all gentoo penguins are the same

children.content: Gentoo penguins were thought to be a standalone species, but a new study says there are four distinct species — and it could lead to better conservation plans.

image.altText: Three small penguins with bright red-orange bills and conspicuous white eye patches.

items.title: Musk's X ordered to pay $750,000 for not complying with Australian child protection request

children.content: Elon ​Musk's social media company X Corp has ​admitted ‌to not complying with an order from Australia's eSafety commissioner to ⁠provide information about the measures it has in place ​to stop ‌child exploitation on the X platform.

image.altText: Elon Musk pouts his lips in a close up portrait

items.title: How Australia's EV uptake compares with the rest of the world

children.content: High petrol prices are driving EV sales around the world, with the leading international authority predicting 30 per cent of all new cars sold this year will be electric.

items.title: Israel supported Ben-Gvir's callousness, until it didn't

children.content: Benjamin Netanyahu has condemned a video of his national security minister taunting activists, including Australians, who have been detained. But it's the prime minister who allowed this behaviour to percolate.

articles.title: Israeli minister posts video taunting detained activists of Global Sumud Flotilla

children.content: Some of the detained activists were being pushed and dragged along the ground by Israeli forces while Itamar Ben-Gvir was taunting them.

image.altText: people kneeling on the ground as armed soldiers watch over

articles.title: Government calls for detained Australian flotilla members to be released from Israel

items.title: Arrow's MND diagnosis shines spotlight on debilitating condition that has no cure

children.content: As another rugby league player is diagnosed with motor neurone disease, we asked experts why so little is known about what causes it.

items.title: As Colbert wraps up The Late Show, late-night TV could be on shaky ground

children.content: Like other checks and balances on presidential power, late-night talk shows — once ratings juggernauts, woven into the fabric of American culture — are being stress-tested. Here's how they became a staging ground for the battle over free speech.

image.altText: A younger Donald Trump, with slicked back hair, speaks to a smiling, younger Stephen Colbert on the set of The Late Show.

items.title: Australian parents in 'survival mode all the time' break taboos on raising kids

items.title: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image has been revealed. Here's why

items.title: Rockhampton hotel fallen into disrepair sells for $5.75 million at auction

children.content: After its closure in 2014, the formerly popular hotel became dilapidated and was frequented by squatters. It has now sold at a court-ordered auction for $5.75 million.

items.title: Ed takes chance another one-in-500-year flood won't hit family home

children.content: It was six months before Ed Trotter could return home after record floods on the NSW Mid North Coast. A year on from the disaster, others are still without working kitchens and bathrooms.
